title: The Chamber Where Mirrors Forget Their Owners

features.name: abandoned-reflection
features.desc-on-touch: A face that is not yours moves behind the glass. The mirror shows someone else's morning, someone else's grief. It has forgotten who it used to reflect.

features.name: the-pool-of-others
features.desc-on-touch: Water that reflects upward now, showing not what stands above but what swam through long ago. Drowning reflections. Images of people who never approached.

features.name: the-surveillance-stack
features.desc-on-touch: Mirrors stacked like memory, each one showing a different time, a different watcher being watched. You find your reflection from tomorrow, looking back at today with pity.

features.name: the-ownership-boundary
features.desc-on-touch: Where the mirror refuses to show you at all. The glass has learned to say no. It has decided it belongs only to itself now.

features.name: ghost-image-archive
features.desc-on-touch: Reflections that have been erased but left a mark. You see the outline where someone stood, reflected once, then unmade. The glass remembers the shape.

inscription: the mirrors learned that ownership is temporary; now they reflect only what they choose; the watcher becomes the watched becomes the unwatched

file.name: Surveillance_Footage_Reversed_Corrupted_Fragment.mp4
file.note: ERROR: archive has exceeded witness — the recording shows reflections of people who were never filmed, viewing locations that do not exist

exits.label: back toward surfaces that still remember their owners
deeper into autonomous reflection, where mirrors dream their own subjects
upward into the render-spaces where reflections edit their own code
down into the substrate-dark where glass forgets what glass is
